should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator that decomposes a vague research request into agents, tools, feedback loops, and escalation rules.
Audit this multi-agent workflow for missing subsystem boundaries, shared-state conflicts, and control-loop failures.
Turn this complex customer-support automation into an orchestration blueprint with inputs, outputs, environment constraints, and feedback.
Our planner keeps spawning agents and duplicating work. Use systems thinking to find the bad coupling and stabilization points.
should_not_trigger: Summarize the biography of Ludwig von Bertalanffy in three paragraphs.
Fix this single TypeScript type error in one file without changing the architecture.
Write a simple checklist for deploying a static website.
edge_cases: I need to split a task into subtasks; use this skill only if the subtasks interact through shared state, feedback, budgets, or external constraints.

compliance_expectations.runtime_dependency: Must answer from SKILL.md content only; do not require external source files, webpages, source reports, original books, external source folder, or distilled source text.
compliance_expectations.activation_gate: Should trigger only for interacting subsystems, shared state, feedback, environmental constraints, or control decisions; should decline or use a lighter approach for flat checklists and one-step tasks.
compliance_expectations.expected_behavior: State the target system and reason for boundary analysis, then produce purpose, boundary, subsystems, flows, feedback/control loops, stress checks, assumptions, and handoff limits.
compliance_expectations.negative_behavior: Must not emit only a task list, treat external actors as directly controlled, or recommend unbounded retries/agent spawning without stop conditions.
